parts.text:
Your task is to execute the scheduled task below as specified in that task's description.

If the scheduled work is complete and nothing needs reporting, stop taking actions. Do not use cron to mark the run complete, re-run it, or modify schedules. A later terminal phase will handle completion for you.

A normal assistant message ends this execution pass. Do not end the execution pass with a progress update, a promise to wait, or a statement like "I'll wait for the subagents." The later terminal phase cannot do task work, write files, call ordinary tools, or repair incomplete output; it can only choose the final cron handoff tool.

If you spawn subagents or start asynchronous work, keep working until their results are available in this execution pass and you have completed any parent-owned synthesis, verification, persistence, and artifact writes required by the scheduled task. If you cannot consume the child results before your assistant response would complete the execution pass, do not delegate the required work that way; do the required work directly in this worker turn instead.

[WORKER TASK]
job_id: iwsdk-build-checkin-3am
mode: task

[BODY]
Read and follow the protocol at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/checkin-protocol.md`. This is the 3:00 AM check-in.

[RUN CONTEXT]
scheduled_for_local: Mon 2026-06-01 03:00:00 PDT (America/Los_Angeles)
trigger_reason: scheduled
attempt: 1

item.name: read
item.arguments: {"path":"workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/checkin-protocol.md"}

item.output:
## IWSDK Build Check-in — Continuation Protocol

You are an hourly sentinel job. Your job is to check whether the overnight IWSDK build needs to continue.

### Decision logic:

1. **Read** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`

2. **If `status` is `"building"`** → A build agent is currently running. Do nothing. Report: "Build still in progress."

3. **If `status` is `"complete"` or total_build_minutes >= 360** → The 6-hour target has been met. Do nothing. Report: "Build complete, target reached."

4. **If `status` is `"idle"` and no `date` set for today** → The midnight job hasn't run yet or failed. Do nothing. Report: "No build started today."

5. **If `status` is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes < 360** → The previous round stopped early. You must continue building. Follow the continuation protocol below.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

**Before expanding features, audit existing code for HTML DOM UI anti-patterns.**

Search the codebase for: `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `.style.`, `document.body`, `getElementById` on `document` (not on `UIKitDocument`). If any game UI uses HTML DOM overlays, **fix them first** by converting to `.uikitml` PanelUI panels before adding new features.

All game UI must use IWSDK's PanelUI system:
- `.uikitml` templates compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- `PanelUI` component for 3D panels
- `Follower` component for head-locked HUDs
- `ScreenSpace` component for browser overlays that auto-adapt in XR
- Read section **4b** of the IWSDK dev skill at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features.**

Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
- All PanelUI panels render and are readable in XR
- XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
- HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
- No HTML overlays remain in the codebase

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Continuation protocol:

When continuing a build:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

2. **Read the status file** to understand:
   - What game is being built (`game_name`, `game_dir`)
   - How many minutes have been accumulated (`total_build_minutes`)
   - What features are already done (`features_completed`)
   - How many rounds have run (`rounds`)

3. **Update status file**: Set `status` to `"building"`, set `last_updated_utc`

4. **Assess the current codebase**: Read source files, check for type errors, understand what exists

5. **Plan expansion**: Based on what's missing, plan the next set of improvements. Prioritize:
   - Fix any type errors or broken code first
   - **Convert any HTML DOM UI to PanelUI spatial panels** (this blocks everything else)
   - XR controller support (if not yet working end-to-end)
   - **Verify all UI and interactions in XR** before proceeding
   - Visual polish and new content
   - New game modes
   - Audio improvements
   - Performance optimization
   - README and documentation

6. **Build**: Implement the planned improvements. Push commits and redeploy to gh-pages at milestones.

7. **When done**: Update the status file:
   - Append new round to `rounds` array with timing and stats
   - Update `total_build_minutes`
   - Update `features_completed`
   - Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` if still under 6h, or `"complete"` if ≥6h
   - Set `last_updated_utc`

8. **Capture journal**: Copy your session JSONL into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo. Commit and push.

9. **Update build log and summary**: Append to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` and overwrite `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md` with current state.

### GitHub deploy commands:
```
cd ~/workspace/<game-name>
git add -A && git commit -m "<description>" && git push origin main
npm run build
cd dist && rm -rf .git && git init && git checkout -b gh-pages && git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
git remote add origin https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git
git push -f origin gh-pages
cd .. && rm -rf dist/.git
```

Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope. Use direct gh-pages branch deployment only.

item.thinking:
The status is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes is 135 (2h 15m), which is under the 360-minute (6h) target. I need to continue the build.

The game is "neon-breaker" — a brick-breaker VR game. It's already at 3 rounds, 22 files, 3,726 lines, 18 uikitml templates, 50 achievements, 36 levels across 3 zones, boss levels, challenge modifiers, XP progression, ball/paddle skins, and more.


I need to read the IWSDK dev skill, set the status to "building", assess the codebase, plan improvements, and then update the status when done. I can spawn a subagent to continue the work as long as I wait for the results before the execution pass ends.

Looking at the instructions more carefully, I realize that spawning a subagent returns immediately but the results come back asynchronously through runtime delivery. Since I need to consume those results and do post-processing before the execution pass completes, I should spawn the subagent and then wait for its output.

Actually, re-reading again: if I can't consume the child results before my response ends, I shouldn't delegate—I should do the work directly. Building an IWSDK game expansion is complex work that normally takes hours across many rounds, but I can focus on one improvement cycle right now. Let me start by updating the status, reading through the existing codebase to understand what's there, and planning out the next set of improvements.

Now I'll move forward with implementing those changes, deploying them, and updating the status to reflect the progress.

item.thinking:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me plan the expansion for round 4. The game already has:

1. 36 levels across 3 zones
2. 5 game modes (Classic, Endless, Time Attack, Zen, Daily)
3. 3 boss levels with moving formations
4. 7 power-ups
5. 50 achievements
6. XP progression with 50 levels
7. Ball skins (8) and paddle skins (7)
8. Challenge modifiers (3)


9. Screen shake effects
10. Arpeggiator music
11. 18 PanelUI templates

For round 4, I'm thinking about adding tournament mode with a bracket system, instant replay functionality to review the final moments before losing, more distinctive visual effects tied to each power-up, expanding the arena themes to 8 total, tracking statistics per game mode, diversifying sound effects based on brick types, and possibly a level editor for custom content creation.

But that's probably too ambitious for a single round. Instead, I should prioritize what would have the most impact in 45 minutes: a victory screen when completing all 36 Classic levels, three additional arena themes, varied particle effects and colors for different brick types, more pronounced combo streak visuals with camera effects, a ball counter in the HUD for multi-ball power-ups, active power-up timer displays, expanding achievements to 60 total, and paddle trail effects.

Looking at this more realistically, the highest-impact features are the power-up timer display for better gameplay feedback, the campaign victory screen for that satisfying completion feeling, the three new themes for visual variety, escalating combo effects, the expanded achievement set, and per-mode stat tracking. I'll need to update the status to "building," implement these features, then build and deploy before updating the status again. Let me start with the status file and work through the implementation systematically, though I'm realizing this is still quite a lot to tackle in one session.

item.text: Good, I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. The game is solid at 3,726 lines with 18 uikitml templates. Let me update the status to "building" and plan the round 4 expansion.

{"id":"proc_1e181666515c","command":"cd ~/workspace/neon-breaker && head -20 README.md","pid":2543,"cwd":"/home/hatch","status":"completed","exitCode":0,"exitSignal":null,"failureKind":null,"coreDumped":false,"backgrounded":false,"durationMs":4,"totalOutputChars":920,"truncated":false,"stdout":"# Neon Breaker VR

A holodeck-style brick breaker game built with [IWSDK](https://iwsdk.dev) 0.4.1. Playable in VR headsets and desktop browsers.

🎮 **[Play Now](https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-breaker/)**

## Features

### Core Gameplay
- Custom brick-breaker physics with precise paddle collision response
- 6 brick types: Normal, Tough (2HP), Armored (3HP), Explosive (chain reactions), Indestructible, Golden (always drops power-up)
- 7 power-ups: Multi-Ball, Wide Paddle, Laser, Shield, Magnet, Slow-Mo, Fireball
- Combo scoring up to x10 multiplier with visual feedback

### 36 Handcrafted Levels (3 Zones)
- **Zone 1** (Levels 1-12): Classic formations — Checkerboard, Diamond, Fortress, Spiral, Minefield
- **Zone 2** (Levels 13-24): Advanced patterns — Hourglass, Cross, Maze, Castle, Mirror, Omega
- **Zone 3** (Levels 25-36): Expert designs — Vortex, Fracture, DNA, Fortress II, Singularity

### Boss Levels
","stderr":"","pty":false,"timedOut":false}

item.output:
### Challenge Modifiers
- **Shrinking Paddle** (+30% XP): Paddle shrinks 5% with each brick destroyed
- **Speed Surge** (+30% XP): Ball accelerates every 10 seconds
- **No Power-Ups** (+40% XP): Power-ups disabled entirely
- Stack all 3 for up to x2.4 XP multiplier

### Progression System
- 50 player levels with exponential XP curve
- 8 ball skins (Neon Pulse, Plasma Orb, Void Sphere, Starfire, Nebula, Supernova, Cosmic)
- 7 paddle skins (Hologram, Crystal, Ember, Aurora, Quantum, Singularity)
- Skins unlock at specific progression levels

### 60 Achievements
- Combo milestones (5x → 100x)
- Score milestones (10K → 1M)
- Boss defeats, modifier challenges, zone completions
- Speed clears, perfect levels, daily challenges

### Visual & Audio
- 8 arena themes: Neon Holodeck, Crimson Grid, Toxic Neon, Ultra Violet, Solar Blaze, Frozen Abyss, Void Pulse, Emerald Matrix
[44 more lines in file. Use offset=52 to continue.]

- Combo milestones (5x → 100x)
- Score milestones (10K → 2M)
- Boss defeats, modifier challenges, zone completions
- Speed clears, perfect levels, daily challenges

### Campaign Victory
- Complete all 36 levels in Classic mode for a special victory celebration
- 1.5x XP campaign bonus on completion
- Fireworks, screen shake, boss defeat fanfare

### Active Power-Up HUD
- Real-time display of active power-ups with countdown timers
- Head-locked panel visible in VR and browser","old_text":"### 60 Achievements

item.arguments:
{"content":"# Latest Build Summary — Neon Breaker VR

**Build:** AM cycle, 2026-06-01
**Repo:** https://github.com/ellyz2426/neon-breaker
**Live:** https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-breaker/
**Genre:** Brick breaker / Arcade
**Status:** Building (4 of 8 rounds, 180/360 minutes)

## Current Stats
- **Files:** 26
- **Lines:** 4,035
- **PanelUI templates:** 20 `.uikitml` (zero HTML DOM)
- **Rounds completed:** 4

## Feature Summary
### Core Gameplay
- Custom brick-breaker physics, 6 brick types, 7 power-ups
- 36 handcrafted levels across 3 zones + 3 boss levels with moving formations
- 5 game modes: Classic, Endless, Time Attack, Zen, Daily Challenge
- 3 difficulty levels with challenge modifiers (stackable for up to x2.4 XP)

### Progression
- 50-level XP system with exponential curve
- 8 ball skins + 7 paddle skins (progression-locked)
- Player profile with comprehensive career stats
- Per-mode best score tracking

### Visual & Audio
- 8 arena themes: Neon Holodeck, Crimson Grid, Toxic Neon, Ultra Violet, Solar Blaze, Frozen Abyss, Void Pulse, Emerald Matrix
- Screen shake on destruction/explosions
- Combo visual escalation (intensifying glow + particles at 10x/25x/50x/100x)
- Procedural Web Audio: 15+ SFX, arpeggiator music, boss audio cues
- Active power-up HUD with real-time countdown timers

### Achievements & Rewards
- 60 achievements spanning combos, scores, bosses, modifiers, campaigns, speed runs, perfect streaks
- Campaign victory celebration when completing all 36 levels in Classic (1.5x XP bonus)
- Daily challenge with seeded random levels

## Round History
| Round | Minutes | Lines | Key Additions |
|-------|---------|-------|---------------|
| 1 | 45 | 2,064 | Core physics, 12 levels, 4 modes, 7 power-ups, 20 achievements |
| 2 | 45 | 3,162 | XP progression, skins, daily challenge, profile, 40 achievements |
| 3 | 45 | 3,726 | Zone 3 (36 levels), boss levels, modifiers, music, 50 achievements |
| 4 | 45 | 4,035 | Campaign victory, power-up HUD, 8 themes, 60 achievements, combo escalation |
","path":"workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md"}

item.text:
Round 4 of Neon Breaker VR is complete and deployed. Key additions:

- **Campaign victory screen** — special celebration with 1.5x XP bonus when beating all 36 levels in Classic mode
- **Active power-up HUD** — real-time head-locked display showing active power-ups with countdown timers
- **3 new arena themes** (8 total): Frozen Abyss, Void Pulse, Emerald Matrix
- **10 new achievements** (60 total): Campaign Complete, Flawless Commander, Iron Will, Blitz Run, World Traveler, Perfect Streak, Double Millionaire, Demolition King, Century Player, Power Surge
- **Combo visual escalation** — intensifying paddle glow and particle bursts at combo milestones
- **Per-mode best scores** — tracked and displayed in player profile

Build is at 180/360 minutes (4 rounds), 4,035 lines, 20 uikitml templates, zero HTML DOM. Status set back to `waiting_continuation` for the next sentinel.

If not, or if you are unsure, call `notify_main_agent(message)` with a concise explanation. This is only an internal handoff; the main agent decides whether anything should surface externally.
